reviewBasis: Recordings are sorted by source modified time. Baseline clips show the original leak; implementation and current-form clips are weighted more because they likely show the feedback being attempted.

mainFeedback.title: Samira: exit first, cash out second
mainFeedback.focus: Newest clips count most: keep naming the exit before E, then stop the fight as soon as the map payoff is available.
mainFeedback.rule: If the first win creates wave, tower, dragon, Baron, nexus, or recall, take it before another duel.
mainFeedback.nextRep: Next queue cue: exit, win, leave.
mainFeedback.whyTrust: It weights the newest attempts most and targets the part that still costs games after the damage is already good.

detectedChampions.name: Samira
detectedChampions.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.
detectedChampions.improvementTitle: E only after the exit exists
detectedChampions.improvement: Hold E until the target is lethal or a reset, Flash, teammate cover, or clean walk-out path is already visible.

recordings.title: highlight 01
recordings.feedbackTitle: E only after the exit exists
recordings.feedback: Hold E until the target is lethal or a reset, Flash, teammate cover, or clean walk-out path is already visible.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira has no real disengage after E; this rule protects the one button that turns winning damage into a death when the exit is missing.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 02
recordings.feedbackTitle: Name the CC before going in
recordings.feedback: Before E/R, identify the one spell that cancels the play; enter only after it is spent, blocked by W, or aimed elsewhere.
recordings.whyTrust: This is the highest-value check because one untracked stun, root, knock-up, or hook turns Samira's whole reset plan off.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 03
recordings.feedback: A low target past vision is not free; shove the wave or take plate unless the next enemy position is known.
recordings.whyTrust: The rule is trustworthy because fog removes the information Samira needs to decide whether the next E is a reset or a bait.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 04
recordings.feedback: After the first kill or forced recall, crash wave first, then choose plate, dragon move, reset, or support roam.
recordings.whyTrust: This is how a won lane becomes rank progress: the enemy loses minions and map space even if no second kill happens.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 05
recordings.feedback: Let Q, autos, and W collect cooldowns before entering; Samira should clean the fight, not start it blind.
recordings.whyTrust: This lowers anxiety without lowering aggression because edge play gathers real cooldown evidence before the all-in.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this baseline recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 06
recordings.feedback: Keep at least five seconds before and after the fight so the trigger, cooldowns, and exit can be judged.
recordings.whyTrust: The advice is low-confidence on gameplay and high-confidence on review quality because a one-second clip hides the decision that caused the outcome.
recordings.evidence: The recording is shorter than three seconds.

recordings.title: highlight 07
recordings.feedback: This is where the new rule starts to matter: after a tower or multi-kill, leave with the shutdown value instead of retesting the fight.
recordings.whyTrust: This is worth practicing because it does not ask for less carry pressure; it keeps the carry gold from being handed back.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this implementation recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 08
recordings.feedback: Treat this as a feedback-rep clip: if a teammate dies nearby, count visible enemies before spending E to rescue a fight.
recordings.whyTrust: Counting visible enemies gives anxiety a concrete check; if the numbers are bad, skipping the rescue is discipline, not fear.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this implementation recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 09
recordings.feedback: Catching side farm is fine; the improvement rep is leaving toward teammates unless mid has priority or three enemies are visible.
recordings.whyTrust: This turns side farm into safe income instead of isolation, which is the difference between carrying with gold and dying with gold.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this implementation recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 10
recordings.feedback: When an enemy catches a wave, pressure the objective first; take the duel only with ult, summoner info, and a walk-out.
recordings.whyTrust: Objectives force the enemy to answer on your terms; random duels make the game hinge on mechanics under uncertainty.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this implementation recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 11
recordings.feedback: This later clip counts more: hold the edge until enemy CC is used so Samira stays the finisher instead of the target.
recordings.whyTrust: This is a current-form adjustment: it preserves the aggression while removing the single easiest way enemies stop Samira.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this current form recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: highlight 12
recordings.feedback: At inhib or nexus, the feedback is already partly working; the next Diamond rep is ending as soon as the structure is available.
recordings.whyTrust: This is reliable because structures are guaranteed progress; extra fighting after the base is open adds variance without adding win condition.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this current form recording by capture order; later clips count more as current form.

recordings.title: full game 8x
recordings.feedback: The later full-game read counts most: keep the exit rule, then leave won fights through tower, dragon, Baron, nexus, or recall.
recordings.whyTrust: This is worth trying because the full-game scoreline shows damage is already present; the remaining climb value is keeping shutdown gold and tempo after wins.
recordings.evidence: Fallback weights this current form recording as the freshest replay evidence.
